title: Lower Handshake to FIRRTL
body:
Add a lowering pass from Handshake to FIRRTL.

This pass can convert most Handshake elastic operations (except for Load, Store, Memory, and EagerFork) and some Standard binary operations (those supported by a FIRRTL binary primitive) to FIRRTL IR. Currently this pass generates pure combinational circuit, thus in the case when graph cycle exists, the generated FIRRTL IR may have incorrect functionality. Buffer, EagerFork, and other timing components will be added soon.

Implementations of all Handshake elastic operations are written manually. When an elastic operation appears, a corresponding sub-module will be created. Then, this sub-module will be instantiated and inserted into the top-module for substituting the original elastic operation.

Some references: Lana's [dynamically scheduled HLS paper](https://www.epfl.ch/labs/lap/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/JosipovicFeb18_DynamicallyScheduledHighLevelSynthesis_FPGA18.pdf) and her [FPGA20 tutorial paper](https://dynamatic.epfl.ch/papers/FPGA_20_Dynamatic_From_C_C_to_Dynamically_Scheduled_Circuits.pdf).
